title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

The Core Information Standard v2.0 (CISV2) defines essential health and care information elements that should be consistently recorded and shared across UK health and social care settings. This standard supports integrated care delivery by establishing a comprehensive framework for capturing person-centred information, from basic demographics through complex care planning and end-of-life preferences.

The standard encompasses mandatory person demographics, GP practice details, and extends to include personal preferences, individual requirements, safeguarding considerations, and comprehensive health records. It emphasises person-centred care through dedicated sections like "About Me" that capture what matters most to individuals receiving care.

## Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder engagement across health and social care sectors, CISV2 builds upon previous NHS information standards while incorporating contemporary requirements for integrated care systems. The standard addresses the critical need for consistent information capture and sharing, supporting the NHS Long Term Plan's vision for joined-up care delivery.

This standard aligns with UK Core FHIR R4 profiles and leverages established clinical terminologies including SNOMED CT, dm+d for medications, and NHS Data Dictionary elements. It provides a structured approach to documenting everything from basic administrative data to complex care plans, ensuring information can flow seamlessly between different care settings while maintaining clinical safety and data quality standards.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Key Health Interventions

The Core Information Standard v2.0 supports documentation of comprehensive health interventions across multiple care domains. The following table summarises key intervention categories and their implementation recommendations:

| Intervention Category | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Reasonable Adjustments** | Record all adjustments required under Equality Act 2010, including communication needs, mobility support, and sensory accommodations | Equality Act 2010, NHS Accessible Information Standard | Mandatory |
| **Medication Management** | Document all medications using dm+d codes, including dose directions, route, and medication changes with clinical rationale | NICE Medicines Optimisation Guidelines | High |
| **Vaccination Records** | Capture vaccine product, procedure, site, route, and any adverse reactions using SNOMED CT terminology | UK Health Security Agency Guidelines | High |
| **Care Planning** | Implement person-centred care plans documenting goals, actions, outcomes, and review dates with agreement from person/representative | NICE Person-Centred Care Guidelines | High |
| **Safeguarding Measures** | Record safeguarding concerns, mental capacity assessments, DoLS applications, and child protection plans with professional accountability | Care Act 2014, Mental Capacity Act 2005 | Mandatory |
| **End of Life Care** | Document CPR decisions, advance care planning, preferred place of death, and anticipatory medications | NICE End of Life Care Guidelines | High |
| **Risk Management** | Identify and document risks to self/others with mitigation strategies and regular review | NHS Risk Management Framework | Medium |
| **Social Care Interventions** | Record services, equipment, adaptations, and support packages with funding sources and review arrangements | Care Act 2014 Guidance | High |

## Implementation Context

These interventions should be recorded with full professional accountability, including performer details, dates, locations (using ODS codes), and regular review arrangements. The standard emphasises capturing both clinical and social care interventions to support integrated care delivery.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## System User Personas

The following personas represent key users of systems implementing the Core Information Standard v2.0:

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | **GP/Primary Care Physician** | Doctor providing first-contact care, managing chronic conditions, and coordinating referrals. Responsible for maintaining comprehensive patient records. | Clinical assessment, care coordination, medication management, SNOMED CT coding, safeguarding responsibilities |
| CISV2.P2 | **Community Nurse** | Registered nurse delivering care in community settings, conducting assessments, administering medications, and supporting care plans. | Clinical procedures, medication administration, care plan implementation, risk assessment, wound care |
| CISV2.P3 | **Social Worker** | Professional assessing social care needs, arranging support services, and ensuring safeguarding compliance. Key role in mental capacity assessments. | Social care assessment, safeguarding procedures, mental capacity assessment, care package coordination |
| CISV2.P4 | **Hospital Consultant** | Senior doctor managing complex conditions, making treatment decisions, and overseeing specialty care including end-of-life planning. | Specialist clinical knowledge, treatment planning, multidisciplinary coordination, prognostication |
| CISV2.P5 | **Care Coordinator** | Professional coordinating integrated care across health and social services, ensuring care plan implementation and review. | Care pathway management, service coordination, review scheduling, stakeholder communication |
| CISV2.P6 | **Allied Health Professional** | Therapist (OT/Physio/SLT) providing specialist assessments, interventions, and equipment recommendations. | Functional assessment, therapy planning, equipment prescription, goal setting |
| CISV2.P7 | **Care Home Manager** | Registered manager overseeing residential care delivery, ensuring regulatory compliance and coordinating with external services. | Care quality management, regulatory compliance, staff supervision, external liaison |
| CISV2.P8 | **Person/Patient Representative** | Individual receiving care or their legitimate representative (LPA holder, parent) participating in care decisions and providing personal information. | Self-advocacy, consent provision, personal preference expression, care participation |

Each persona interacts with different sections of the standard based on their professional responsibilities and access permissions.

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Scenario 1: Integrated Care Planning Following Hospital Discharge

**Context:** Mrs Thompson, 78, is being discharged from hospital following a fall and hip fracture. She has mild dementia, diabetes, and lives alone.

**Actors:** Hospital Consultant (CISV2.P4), Social Worker (CISV2.P3), Community Nurse (CISV2.P2), Care Coordinator (CISV2.P5)

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Multidisciplinary discharge planning meeting |
| **Information Captured** | Discharge summary, mobility assessment, cognitive status, home environment risks, medication changes, care package requirements |
| **Key Interactions** | Consultant documents clinical status and prognosis; Social worker completes needs assessment and arranges care package; Care coordinator develops integrated care plan with goals |
| **Outcomes** | Comprehensive discharge record created; Care plan agreed with family; Equipment ordered; Follow-up appointments scheduled |
| **Data Elements Used** | Admission/discharge details, problem list, procedures, medications, assessments, care plan, equipment, professional contacts |

## Scenario 2: Reasonable Adjustment Implementation

**Context:** James, 35, has autism and learning disabilities. He needs a routine dental procedure but requires specific adjustments for healthcare access.

**Actors:** GP (CISV2.P1), Person/Representative (CISV2.P8), Hospital teams

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | GP referral for dental surgery noting need for reasonable adjustments |
| **Information Captured** | Communication needs (easy read, extra time), sensory sensitivities, support requirements, preferred coping strategies |
| **Key Interactions** | GP documents adjustments in referral; Person contributes "About Me" information; Hospital team reviews requirements pre-admission |
| **Outcomes** | Adjustments flagged across systems; Pre-visit arranged; Quiet room allocated; Support plan implemented |
| **Data Elements Used** | Individual requirements (reasonable adjustments, impairments), About Me section, alerts, care preferences |

## Scenario 3: End-of-Life Care Coordination

**Context:** Mr Patel, 68, has advanced lung cancer. Following discussions with his consultant, he wishes to plan his end-of-life care.

**Actors:** Hospital Consultant (CISV2.P4), GP (CISV2.P1), Community Nurse (CISV2.P2), Patient (CISV2.P8)

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Prognosis discussion and patient request for advance care planning |
| **Information Captured** | CPR decision (DNACPR), preferred place of death (home), advance statement of wishes, anticipatory medications |
| **Key Interactions** | Consultant documents prognosis and initial discussions; GP coordinates anticipatory prescribing; Community nurse ensures home equipment available |
| **Outcomes** | ReSPECT form completed; Anticipatory medications prescribed; Care preferences shared across services; Family aware of plans |
| **Data Elements Used** | End of life care section, advance decisions, contingency plans, medications, professional contacts |

These scenarios demonstrate how the standard supports complex, multi-professional care delivery while maintaining person-centred documentation.

title: References
content:
## References

### Legislation and Statutory Guidance

1. **Equality Act 2010**. UK Public General Acts. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents

2. **Mental Capacity Act 2005**. UK Public General Acts. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents

3. **Care Act 2014**. UK Public General Acts. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2014/23/contents

4. **Mental Health Act 1983 (as amended 2007)**. UK Public General Acts. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1983/20/contents

### NHS Standards and Guidance

5. **NHS Data Dictionary**. NHS Digital. Available at: https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/

6. **UK Core FHIR Implementation Guide**. NHS Digital. Available at: https://simplifier.net/guide/uk-core-implementation-guide

7. **dm+d - Dictionary of Medicines and Devices**. NHS Business Services Authority. Available at: https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd

8. **Organisation Data Service (ODS)**. NHS Digital. Available at: https://digital.nhs.uk/services/organisation-data-service

9. **NHS Accessible Information Standard**. NHS England. Available at: https://www.england.nhs.uk/about/equality/equality-hub/patient-equalities-programme/equality-frameworks-and-information-standards/accessibleinfo/

### Clinical Terminology

10. **SNOMED CT UK Edition**. NHS Digital. Available at: https://digital.nhs.uk/services/terminology-and-classifications/snomed-ct

11. **LOINC**. Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes. Regenstrief Institute. Available at: https://loinc.org/

12. **UCUM - Unified Code for Units of Measure**. Regenstrief Institute. Available at: https://unitsofmeasure.org/

### Clinical Guidelines

13. **NICE Guidelines - Medicines Optimisation (NG5)**. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. 2015. Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng5

14. **NICE Guidelines - End of Life Care for Adults (QS13)**. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. 2021. Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/qs13

15. **NICE Guidelines - People's Experience in Adult Social Care (QS182)**. National Institute for Health and Care Excellence. 2019. Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/qs182

### Professional Standards

16. **Professional Standards for Record Keeping**. Nursing and Midwifery Council. 2018. Available at: https://www.nmc.org.uk/standards/

17. **Good Medical Practice**. General Medical Council. 2019. Available at: https://www.gmc-uk.org/professional-standards/professional-standards-for-doctors/good-medical-practice

18. **Standards of Proficiency**. Health and Care Professions Council. 2023. Available at: https://www.hcpc-uk.org/standards/standards-of-proficiency/

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content: The following business processes demonstrate how different personas interact with the Core Information Standard to deliver integrated care. Each process captures critical data elements and supports information flow between health and social care professionals.

processes_data.summary_markdown: The following business processes demonstrate how different personas interact with the Core Information Standard to deliver integrated care. Each process captures critical data elements and supports information flow between health and social care professionals.

activities.title: Reasonable Adjustments Identification and Recording
activities.personas: Social Worker, GP/Primary Care Physician, Care Coordinator
activities.objectives: To identify, document and share reasonable adjustments required under the Equality Act 2010, ensuring all services can provide accessible care.

activities.title: Safeguarding Concern Management
activities.personas: Social Worker, GP/Primary Care Physician, Hospital Consultant, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: To identify, assess, document and manage safeguarding concerns in accordance with statutory requirements.

activities.title: Integrated Care Planning
activities.personas: Care Coordinator, GP/Primary Care Physician, Social Worker, Allied Health Professional, Person/Patient Representative
activities.objectives: To develop person-centred care plans that integrate health and social care needs, with clear goals and actions.

activities.title: Medication Management Across Settings
activities.personas: GP/Primary Care Physician, Hospital Consultant, Community Nurse, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: To ensure safe medication management during transitions of care with complete reconciliation.

activities.title: End of Life Care Planning
activities.personas: Hospital Consultant, GP/Primary Care Physician, Community Nurse, Person/Patient Representative, Care Coordinator
activities.objectives: To sensitively plan and document end of life care preferences ensuring dignity and respect for person's wishes.

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Core Data Elements

The following table summarises the essential data elements within the Core Information Standard v2.0, grouped by domain. Each element supports integrated care delivery through consistent, structured information capture.

### Person Demographics & Identity

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | Person Name | Full structured name including first, family, preferred names | Complex | Mandatory | NHS_DD Person name |
| CISV2.DE2 | Date of Birth | Person's date of birth | Date | Mandatory | NHS_DD Person birth date |
| CISV2.DE3 | NHS Number | Unique NHS identifier | String(10) | Required | NHS_DD NHS number |
| CISV2.DE4 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String(1) | Required | NHS_DD Person stated gender code |
| CISV2.DE5 | Address | Structured address including postcode | Complex | Mandatory | NHS_DD Patient usual address |
| CISV2.DE6 | Contact Details | Phone, email, preferred contact method | Complex | Required | NHS_DD UK telephone number |
| CISV2.DE7 | GP Practice Details | Registered GP practice information | Complex | Mandatory | ODS code |

### Individual Requirements & Accessibility

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE8 | Reasonable Adjustment Flag | Indicator for required adjustments | Boolean | Required | SNOMED 1108111000000107 |
| CISV2.DE9 | Reasonable Adjustment Details | Specific adjustments needed | Complex | Required | SNOMED Accessible Information refsets |
| CISV2.DE10 | Impairment Category | Type of impairment requiring support | Coded | Required | SNOMED CT coded |
| CISV2.DE11 | Communication Needs | How person communicates | Text | Required | Free text/coded |
| CISV2.DE12 | Mobility Needs | Movement and access requirements | Coded | Required | SNOMED mobility findings refset |

### Clinical Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE13 | Problem List | Active health conditions/issues | Complex | Mandatory | SNOMED Health issues refset |
| CISV2.DE14 | Allergies/Reactions | Known allergies and adverse reactions | Complex | Mandatory | dm+d/SNOMED substances |
| CISV2.DE15 | Medications | Current medication list | Complex | Mandatory | dm+d VTM/VMP/AMP |
| CISV2.DE16 | Vaccinations | Immunisation records | Complex | Required | dm+d vaccine products |
| CISV2.DE17 | Procedures | Clinical procedures performed | Complex | Required | SNOMED procedures |

### Safeguarding & Legal

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE18 | Safeguarding Indicator | Flag for safeguarding concerns | Boolean | Required | Yes/No flag |
| CISV2.DE19 | Safeguarding Concerns | Specific safeguarding issues | Coded | Required | SNOMED Safeguarding refset |
| CISV2.DE20 | Mental Capacity Assessment | Capacity decision records | Complex | Required | Free text/structured |
| CISV2.DE21 | LPA Status | Lasting Power of Attorney details | Complex | Required | SNOMED LPA findings refset |
| CISV2.DE22 | DoLS Status | Deprivation of Liberty status | Complex | Required | Requested/Authorised |

### Care Planning & Coordination

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE23 | Care Plan Goals | Person's goals and hopes | Complex | Required | SNOMED/Free text |
| CISV2.DE24 | Care Plan Actions | Planned activities and interventions | Complex | Required | SNOMED procedures |
| CISV2.DE25 | Professional Contacts | Care team members | Complex | Required | SDSJobRole/ODS codes |
| CISV2.DE26 | Personal Contacts | Family/friend contacts | Complex | Mandatory | Free text structured |
| CISV2.DE27 | Services & Care | Active care packages | Complex | Required | Free text/Local codes |

### End of Life Care

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE28 | CPR Decision | Resuscitation status | Coded | Required | SNOMED 450475007/450476008 |
| CISV2.DE29 | Preferred Place of Death | Person's preference | Complex | Required | SCC11580 codes |
| CISV2.DE30 | Estimated Prognosis | Clinical prognosis | Text | Required | Free text |
| CISV2.DE31 | Advance Statement | Advance care preferences | Complex | Required | SNOMED 816281000000101 |

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Decision-Support Logic

The Core Information Standard v2.0 incorporates decision-support logic to guide appropriate care delivery based on person circumstances and requirements. The following decision tables support key clinical and operational decisions.

### Table 1: Reasonable Adjustment Requirements

**Purpose:** Determine required adjustments based on impairment type and individual needs

| Input Conditions | Output Action |
|---|---|
| **Impairment Category** = Sensory (Hearing) AND **Preferred Communication** = British Sign Language | Provide BSL interpreter for all appointments; Use visual alerts; Provide written information |
| **Impairment Category** = Sensory (Visual) AND **Information Format** = Large print | Provide all documentation in minimum 16pt font; Ensure good lighting; Describe procedures verbally |
| **Impairment Category** = Learning Disability AND **Support Level** = High | Allow extra time (double appointments); Use easy-read materials; Ensure support person present |
| **Impairment Category** = Autism AND **Sensory Sensitivity** = Present | Provide quiet waiting area; Minimise sudden changes; Give clear advance warning of procedures |
| **Mobility Needs** = Wheelchair user | Ensure step-free access; Height-adjustable examination couches; Accessible toilet facilities |
| **Communication Needs** = Non-verbal AND **Uses AAC** = Yes | Support use of communication aids; Allow extra time; Train staff in AAC awareness |

### Table 2: Safeguarding Response Decisions

**Purpose:** Determine appropriate safeguarding response based on concern type and person circumstances

| Input Conditions | Output Action |
|---|---|
| **Person Age** < 18 AND **Safeguarding Concern** = Physical abuse | Immediate referral to Children's Services; Document injuries; Consider police involvement |
| **Person Age** ≥ 18 AND **Mental Capacity** = Lacks capacity AND **Concern** = Financial abuse | Adult safeguarding referral; Consider DoLS application; Notify Office of Public Guardian if LPA |
| **Safeguarding Indicator** = Yes AND **Risk to Others** = Yes | Multi-agency risk assessment conference (MARAC); Safety planning; Consider emergency measures |
| **Looked After Child** = Yes AND **New Concern** = Yes | Notify social worker within 24 hours; Update care plan; Review placement suitability |
| **Mental Health Act Status** = Detained AND **Concern** = Self-neglect | Involve Mental Health team; Review treatment plan; Consider capacity assessment |

### Table 3: Medication Reconciliation Rules

**Purpose:** Guide medication reconciliation decisions during care transitions

| Input Conditions | Output Action |
|---|---|
| **Admission** = Yes AND **Medication Count** > 5 | Full medication reconciliation required; Pharmacist review within 24 hours |
| **Allergy Status** = Multiple drug allergies AND **New Medication** = Yes | Check all ingredients against allergy list; Consider alternative formulations; Document clearly |
| **Renal Function** = Impaired AND **Medication** = Renally excreted | Adjust dose per eGFR; Monitor levels if applicable; Review at each transition |
| **Age** > 75 AND **Anticholinergic Burden** = High | Review for deprescribing opportunities; Monitor for confusion; Consider alternatives |
| **Discharge** = Yes AND **Changes Made** = Yes | Provide medication list to patient; Notify GP within 24 hours; Arrange follow-up |

### Table 4: Care Coordination Triggers

**Purpose:** Identify when enhanced care coordination is required

| Input Conditions | Output Action |
|---|---|
| **Hospital Admissions** ≥ 3 in 6 months | Refer to Care Coordinator; Comprehensive assessment; Consider virtual ward |
| **Professional Contacts** > 5 active AND **No Key Worker** = True | Assign Care Coordinator; Create integrated care plan; Schedule MDT meeting |
| **Mental Capacity** = Fluctuating AND **Living Alone** = Yes | Social care assessment; Consider support package; Review safeguarding |
| **End of Life** = Yes AND **Preferred Place** ≠ Current location | Urgent care coordination; Fast-track continuing care; Coordinate with all services |
| **Complex Needs** = Yes AND **Carer Stress** = High | Carer's assessment; Respite options; Additional support services |

### Table 5: End of Life Care Decisions

**Purpose:** Guide end of life care planning based on prognosis and preferences

| Input Conditions | Output Action |
|---|---|
| **Prognosis** = Days AND **Preferred Place of Death** = Home AND **In Hospital** = Yes | Fast-track discharge planning; Arrange 24/7 district nursing; Anticipatory medications |
| **CPR Decision** = Not for resuscitation AND **Family Aware** = No | Sensitive discussion with family; Document reasoning; Offer support |
| **Prognosis** = Weeks to months AND **Advance Planning** = None | Initiate advance care planning; Discuss preferences; Document wishes |
| **Deteriorating** = Yes AND **Anticipatory Meds** = Not available | Prescribe anticipatory medications; Ensure 24/7 availability; Brief all carers |
| **Preferred Place** = Hospice AND **Bed Available** = No | Add to urgent waiting list; Optimise symptom control; Consider alternatives |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

The following indicators support monitoring of Core Information Standard v2.0 implementation and its impact on care quality and integration.

### Quality of Care Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND1 | Reasonable Adjustments Recorded | Number of people with reasonable adjustments fully documented | Total number of people with identified adjustment needs | By adjustment type, organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND2 | Person-Centred Information Completeness | Number of people with completed 'About Me' sections | Total number of people in active care | By care setting, age group | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND3 | Care Plan Agreement Rate | Number of care plans with documented person/representative agreement | Total number of care plans created | By service type, capacity status | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND4 | Medication Reconciliation Timeliness | Number of medication reconciliations completed within 24 hours of admission | Total number of admissions | By admission type, ward | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND5 | Safeguarding Response Time | Number of safeguarding concerns with response initiated within required timeframe | Total number of safeguarding concerns raised | By concern type, person age | Monthly |

### Integration & Coordination Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND6 | Multi-Professional Access | Number of unique professionals accessing person's record | Total number of people with active care plans | By professional role, organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND7 | Information Sharing Completeness | Number of care transitions with complete information transfer | Total number of care transitions | By transition type, organisations | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND8 | Care Coordinator Assignment | Number of people with complex needs assigned a care coordinator | Total number of people meeting complexity criteria | By locality, need type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND9 | Cross-Sector Care Planning | Number of care plans with both health and social care input | Total number of care plans | By lead organisation, funding | Quarterly |

### Safety & Risk Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND10 | Allergy Alert Effectiveness | Number of prescriptions checked against recorded allergies | Total number of prescriptions issued | By prescriber type, setting | Daily |
| CISV2.IND11 | Risk Assessment Currency | Number of people with risk assessments reviewed within required timeframe | Total number of people with identified risks | By risk type, review period | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND12 | Mental Capacity Documentation | Number of significant decisions with capacity assessment documented | Total number of significant decisions recorded | By decision type, outcome | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND13 | DoLS Compliance | Number of DoLS applications submitted within statutory timeframe | Total number of people identified as potentially deprived of liberty | By care setting, urgency | Weekly |

### Person Experience Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND14 | Preferred Place of Care Achievement | Number of people receiving care in their preferred place | Total number with expressed preference | By care type, location | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND15 | Communication Needs Met | Number of appointments where documented communication needs were met | Total appointments for people with communication needs | By need type, service | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND16 | End of Life Preferences Honoured | Number achieving preferred place of death | Total deaths where preference was recorded | By location type, advance notice | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND17 | Care Plan Goal Achievement | Number of care plan goals marked as achieved | Total number of goals set | By goal type, timeframe | Quarterly |

### System Performance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND18 | Data Quality Score | Number of mandatory fields completed correctly | Total number of mandatory fields | By section, organisation | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND19 | Professional Record Compliance | Number of entries with complete professional identification | Total number of professional entries | By role, organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND20 | Coding Accuracy | Number of clinical entries with appropriate SNOMED/dm+d codes | Total number of codeable clinical entries | By domain, user group | Monthly |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

Systems implementing the Core Information Standard v2.0 must meet the following functional and non-functional requirements to ensure effective integrated care delivery.

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR1 | Person Demographics Management | System must capture, validate and maintain all mandatory person demographic elements including NHS number verification, structured name and address components | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR2 | Reasonable Adjustments Recording | System must support flagging, detailed recording and sharing of reasonable adjustments with SNOMED coding for adjustment types and impairments | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR3 | Multi-Professional Access Control | System must enable role-based access for all defined personas with appropriate permissions based on legitimate relationships and care involvement | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR4 | Care Plan Development | System must support collaborative care planning with goals, actions, responsibilities and person agreement tracking with version control | High |
| CISV2.FR5 | Medication Reconciliation | System must enable medication reconciliation across care transitions with dm+d coding, allergy checking and change documentation | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR6 | Safeguarding Workflow | System must support safeguarding workflows including flagging, assessment recording, statutory reporting and multi-agency coordination | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR7 | Clinical Coding Support | System must support SNOMED CT coding for clinical findings, dm+d for medications, and LOINC for observations with search and validation | High |
| CISV2.FR8 | Document Management | System must store and retrieve associated documents including advance decisions, care plans and clinical correspondence with appropriate metadata | High |
| CISV2.FR9 | End of Life Planning | System must capture CPR decisions, preferred place of death/care, advance care planning with sensitivity flags and controlled sharing | High |
| CISV2.FR10 | Audit Trail | System must maintain complete audit trail of all access and changes with professional identification, timestamps and justification where required | Mandatory |
| CISV2.FR11 | Information Sharing | System must support standards-based information exchange using UK Core FHIR R4 profiles for all defined data elements | High |
| CISV2.FR12 | Person Access | System must provide person/representative access to view and contribute to 'About Me' and care planning sections with appropriate controls | Medium |
| CISV2.FR13 | Risk Assessment Management | System must support structured risk assessment recording, mitigation planning and review scheduling with alerts for overdue reviews | High |
| CISV2.FR14 | Professional Registration | System must validate professional registration details against national registers where available and maintain current contact information | High |
| CISV2.FR15 | Reporting & Analytics | System must generate all defined performance indicators with required disaggregation and support custom queries on the data model | Medium |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR1 | Availability | System must maintain 99.9% availability during core hours (8am-8pm) with planned maintenance outside these hours | Mandatory |
| CISV2.NFR2 | Response Time | System must return person record queries within 2 seconds and complete saves within 3 seconds under normal load conditions | High |
| CISV2.NFR3 | Scalability | System must support minimum 100,000 active person records and 500 concurrent users without performance degradation | High |
| CISV2.NFR4 | Security | System must implement NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit standards including encryption at rest and in transit, secure authentication | Mandatory |
| CISV2.NFR5 | Clinical Safety | System must comply with DCB0129 and DCB0160 clinical safety standards with documented hazard assessment and mitigation | Mandatory |
| CISV2.NFR6 | Accessibility | System must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards and support assistive technologies for users with disabilities | Mandatory |
| CISV2.NFR7 | Interoperability | System must implement UK Core FHIR R4 profiles and support standard NHS integration patterns including spine services | Mandatory |
| CISV2.NFR8 | Data Retention | System must retain records in line with NHS Records Management Code of Practice with appropriate archival and deletion capabilities | High |
| CISV2.NFR9 | Business Continuity | System must maintain local resilience with maximum 4-hour recovery time objective and 1-hour recovery point objective | High |
| CISV2.NFR10 | Mobile Access | System must provide responsive design supporting tablets and smartphones for community and ward-based care delivery | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR11 | Offline Capability | System must support offline access to core information with synchronisation when connectivity restored for community settings | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR12 | Training & Support | System must include role-based training modules and context-sensitive help meeting NHS accessibility standards | High |
| CISV2.NFR13 | Configuration | System must support local configuration of workflows, forms and terminology without requiring code changes | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR14 | Performance Monitoring | System must provide real-time monitoring of system performance, data quality and user activity with alerting capabilities | High |
| CISV2.NFR15 | Consent Management | System must track and enforce information sharing preferences with granular control over section visibility and override audit | Mandatory |
